findings.evidence: `rg -n "theorem prod .*IsCompl|prod_inf_prod|prod_sup_prod"` shows the declaration at `TotallyReal.lean:44` and its proof uses only `Submodule.prod_inf_prod`, `Submodule.prod_sup_prod`, `hL.inf_eq_bot`, and `hL.sup_eq_top`, with no `IsMaximalTotallyReal` dependency.
findings.fix: Move this lemma to a generic linear-algebra support file such as `TauCeti/LinearAlgebra/Prod.lean` or `TauCeti/LinearAlgebra/Projection.lean`, then import that file from `TotallyReal.lean`.
findings.issue: `TauCeti.IsCompl.prod` is a broadly useful product-of-complements lemma, not totally-real-specific material, but it is hidden in `TotallyReal.lean`.

summary: One generic complement-product lemma is placed inside the totally-real file even though it has no totally-real hypotheses and only depends on product/complement lattice API. The totally-real definitions and imports otherwise sit in a natural linear-algebra location.
